title: How to Register a Business Name in Nigeria (2025 CAC Guide)
excerpt: A complete step-by-step guide to registering your business name with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) in Nigeria — what you need, how long it takes, and what it costs.
content:
What is a Business Name Registration?

Registering a business name (also called a sole proprietorship or partnership registration) is the simplest and most affordable way to formalize your business in Nigeria under the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA 2020).

Who Should Register a Business Name?

  • Freelancers and consultants
  • Small traders and artisans
  • Startups testing a business idea
  • Partnerships of two or more individuals

Step-by-Step Registration Process

The CAC has fully digitized the business name registration process. Here's how it works:

  1. Name Availability Search — Check that your desired name is not already taken on the CAC portal.
  2. Complete CAC Form — Provide proprietor details, business nature, and address.
  3. Upload Documents — Valid ID (NIN, passport, or driver's license), passport photograph, proof of address.
  4. Pay Fees — Government filing fees plus professional service charges.
  5. Receive Certificate — Your Certificate of Registration is issued digitally within 24–48 hours.

Cost of Business Name Registration

At Paul & Associates, business name registration starts at ₦45,000 — inclusive of all CAC government fees and our professional service charge. No hidden extras.

How Long Does It Take?

With complete documentation, business name registration typically takes 24–48 hours from submission to certificate issuance.

Ready to register? Contact us on WhatsApp and we'll guide you through the entire process — stress-free.
author: Paul & Associates

title: Business Name vs. Limited Company in Nigeria: Which is Right for You?
excerpt: Confused about whether to register a business name or incorporate a limited liability company? This guide breaks down the key differences, advantages, and when to choose each structure.
content:
The Two Most Common Business Structures in Nigeria

When starting a business in Nigeria, the two most common registration options are a Business Name (sole proprietorship/partnership) and a Private Limited Company (Ltd). Choosing the right structure from the start can save you time, money, and legal headaches.

Business Name Registration

A business name registration under CAMA 2020 is ideal for small-scale businesses. Key features include:

  • Simple and affordable to register (₦45,000 with Paul & Associates)
  • No separate legal personality — you and the business are the same entity
  • Personal liability for business debts
  • Cannot raise capital through share issuance
  • Best for sole traders, freelancers, and informal SMEs

Private Limited Company (Ltd)

A Private Limited Liability Company (RC number) is a separate legal entity distinct from its owners. Benefits include:

  • Limited liability — personal assets protected from business debts
  • Can raise capital through shares (up to 50 shareholders)
  • More credible for contracts, bank loans, and government tenders
  • Can own property and sue/be sued independently
  • Starts at ₦100,000 with Paul & Associates

Which Should You Choose?

Choose a Business Name if: you're a solo freelancer, a small trader, or just testing a business idea with minimal investment.

Choose a Limited Company if: you plan to scale, attract investors, win government contracts, or need limited liability protection.

Not sure which is best for your situation? Chat with us on WhatsApp — we provide free advisory to help you choose the right structure.
author: Paul & Associates

title: CAC Annual Returns Filing in Nigeria: Avoid Penalties and Strike-Off
excerpt: Every registered company in Nigeria must file annual returns with the CAC. Failure to do so attracts penalties and can result in your company being struck off. Here's everything you need to know.
content:
What are Annual Returns?

Annual returns are mandatory statutory filings that every company registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) must submit each year. They confirm that the company is still active and provide updated information about directors, shareholders, and the company's address.

Who Must File Annual Returns?

  • All Business Names (sole proprietorships and partnerships)
  • All Private Limited Companies (Ltd)
  • All Public Limited Companies (PLC)
  • All Incorporated Trustees (NGOs and associations)

Penalties for Late Filing

The CAC imposes penalties for late or non-filing of annual returns:

  • Business Names: ₦5,000 per year of default
  • Limited Companies: ₦10,000 per year of default (plus ₦2,500 per day for continuing default)
  • NGOs: Penalties vary; can result in loss of incorporation status

What Happens If You Don't File?

Persistent failure to file annual returns can result in your company being struck off the CAC register. A struck-off company loses its legal status and cannot enter into contracts, open bank accounts, or operate legally.

How We Can Help

At Paul & Associates, we handle annual returns filing for companies of all sizes. We track your filing deadlines, prepare all required documents, and submit on your behalf — keeping your company in good standing.

Don't wait until penalties accumulate. Contact us today to file your outstanding annual returns.
author: Paul & Associates
